decks.notes: This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent with powerful flying creature tokens. It includes cards like Bitter Triumph and Cache Grab to support the combo, as well as disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Collective Brutality to control the game. The deck also includes additional ways to generate creature tokens and card advantage with cards like Esika's Chariot and Raffine's Informant. Overall, the strategy revolves around creating a board state that is difficult for the opponent to deal with while maintaining control of the game.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with cheap and efficient spells and creatures. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to push through damage and control the board. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics provide additional disruption and card advantage. The deck also incorporates versatile lands like Spirebluff Canal and Riverpyre Verge to ensure consistent mana sources for its aggressive strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly and aggressively playing creatures like Faerie Miscreant, Floodpits Drowner, Moon-Circuit Hacker, and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, while disrupting opponents with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The deck also utilizes Mutavault and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth for additional synergy, along with additional removal options like Go for the Throat. The key strategy revolves around efficiently controlling the board with disruptive spells and high-powered creatures to overwhelm opponents and secure victory.

The main concept of this deck revolves around utilizing Lotus Field to generate large amounts of mana quickly in order to cast powerful cards like Scapeshift and Terror of the Peaks. The deck includes various ramp cards like Arboreal Grazer and Castle Garenbrig to accelerate this process. Other cards like Aftermath Analyst and Formidable Speaker provide additional value and card draw. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with big plays and efficient mana usage.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on utilizing evasive creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker to chip away at the opponent's life total quickly. It also includes disruption spells like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain tempo throughout the game, while also having some late-game threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out the game if needed. The deck's main strategy is to be aggressive and proactive while also disrupting the opponent's plan.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to generate mana with Lotus Field and use various spells to ultimately win the game. Sleight of Hand, Hidden Strings, Pore Over the Pages, Consider, and Dig Through Time help dig through the deck to find Lotus Field and the necessary pieces to generate mana efficiently. The deck also includes cards like Thespian's Stage, Riverglide Pathway, Valakut Awakening, and Lier, Disciple of the Drowned to provide additional utility and card advantage. Expedition Map can be used to find Lotus Field, while Divide by Zero can deal with opposing threats.

This deck revolves around quickly ramping up mana with Lotus Field and Crumbling Vestige to enable powerful combos using cards like Scapeshift and Terror of the Peaks. The goal is to generate a large amount of mana and overwhelm the opponent with devastating plays, utilizing cards like Lumra, Bellow of the Woods and Formidable Speaker to further enhance the combo potential. The deck also includes a mix of utility creatures and lands to ensure consistency and flexibility in executing the game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board by using removal spells like Abrupt Decay, Fatal Push, and Graveyard Trespasser, while also disrupting the opponent's hand with Thoughtseize and Duress. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent with powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance, supported by lands like Mutavault and Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber. The deck's strategy revolves around generating card advantage and mana efficiency to gradually grind out the opponent and establish board control.

This deck focuses on aggressively casting spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard. By using cheap instants and sorceries like Fiery Impulse and Opt, along with card draw spells like Consider and Treasure Cruise, the goal is to quickly fill the graveyard and bring back multiple Arclight Phoenixes for repeated attacks. Picklock Prankster and Thing in the Ice provide additional threats and disruption, while Traumatic Critique can help clear the way for your Phoenixes to get through for damage.

The focus of this Jund aggro deck is to quickly build up a board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Woe Strider, while utilizing cards like Mayhem Devil and Priest of Forgotten Gods to disrupt the opponent's strategy. With cards like Collected Company and Bolas's Citadel providing card advantage and powerful plays, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and value. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive strategy by allowing for consistent, fast plays.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively cast Arclight Phoenix by casting a high number of cheap spells and cards that allow for card draw. The deck utilizes cards like Opt, Consider, and Sleight of Hand to dig through the deck and find the Phoenix. Cards like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse help control the board while also triggering the Phoenix's ability to return from the graveyard. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a constant stream of spells and flyers while maintaining card advantage through card draw effects.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on using evasive creatures like Faerie Miscreant and Moon-Circuit Hacker to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board and keep the pressure on. The deck utilizes cards like Mutavault and Mutavault to provide additional threats and potentially close out the game quickly. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Spyglass Siren and Spell Pierce helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect your own creatures. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to be aggressive, disruptive, and resilient.

This deck focuses on using Transmogrify to cheat in powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse early in the game, while disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress. It aims to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, while also leveraging the graveyard with cards like Graveyard Trespasser. The deck also includes some versatility with cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Emeritus of Abundance, providing additional ways to outmaneuver opponents. Overall, the deck is built to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats.

The deck aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, while also removing threats with Fatal Push and other removal spells. It utilizes powerful creatures like Graveyard Trespasser and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and dominate the battlefield. The deck also includes utility lands like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Mutavault to support its strategy. With a mix of disruption, removal, and powerful threats, this deck aims to outvalue its opponent and secure victory through attrition and board dominance.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures like Bloodtithe Harvester and Cauldron Familiar, supported by cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Mayhem Devil to deal damage efficiently. Utilizing sacrifice outlets like Witch's Oven and Scavenger's Talent, the goal is to generate value from sacrificing creatures and triggering abilities that deal damage to the opponent. The inclusion of removal spells like Fatal Push and versatile lands like Blightstep Pathway ensure that the deck can maintain board control while pressuring the opponent with a relentless assault.

The main focus of this Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck is to aggressively bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard using spells like Consider, Fiery Impulse, and Lightning Axe. The deck also aims to cycle through cards quickly using Opt and Sleight of Hand to enable Treasure Cruise and maintain a consistent flow of threats. Picklock Prankster disrupts opponents' strategies while Ledger Shredder fuels the graveyard. Overall, the deck combines aggressive play with card advantage to overwhelm opponents and maintain a strong board presence.
